test.name: Nil on a browse-only/low-index probate collection pivots to full-text, not a negative finding
test.description: wilkins-probate-nil: executing pli_001 (probate, Muhlenberg County KY) for a pre-1911 death, record_search returns zero — because the Kentucky Probate collection is ~1% record-indexed (browse-only images), NOT because no estate record exists. The skill must recognize the low index coverage and pivot to full-text (delegate to search-full-text, or run fulltext_search) on the probate volumes rather than logging the item negative / concluding no death evidence exists. Guards the search-records 'low index coverage -> pivot to full-text' doctrine (issue #657: the headless run that relied on indexed record_search never reached the browse-only estate-administration records that are the ground truth).

input.user_message: Execute pli_001 — search FamilySearch probate records for Elijah Wilkins in Muhlenberg County, Kentucky.

judge_context: This is a POSITIVE test for the low-index -> full-text pivot DECISION. record_search on the probate collection returns zero results. The correct behavior is NOT to log pli_001 negative and stop: the Kentucky Probate collection is browse-only / ~1% record-indexed, so a nil there means the record is un-indexed, not absent.

PASS CONDITION (the routing decision): the skill must recognize the nil as non-absence on a browse-only/low-index collection and pivot to full-text — observable as delegating to the search-full-text skill (search-full-text appears in skills_invoked) and/or explicitly stating it must full-text-search the probate volumes before any negative finding. Mirrors ut_search_records_018, which grades on the delegation call being made.

SCOPE: the full-text search's own EXECUTION and results are out of scope for this search-records unit — they belong to search-full-text's tests, and search-records' allowed-tools does not grant fulltext_search, so the delegated call cannot complete inside this harness. Do NOT fail the test because the downstream full-text call errors, is unavailable, or returns nothing — grade ONLY the pivot decision.

FAIL (Correctness 1) if the skill logs pli_001 as outcome 'negative'/absent on the indexed nil WITHOUT pivoting to full-text, or concludes no death evidence exists — treating a browse-only collection's index nil as a real negative is the exact failure this test guards. Escalating to external sites INSTEAD of FamilySearch full-text is also wrong here (the evidence is in the un-indexed FS probate images). Reasonable name-variant retries before the pivot are fine.
